title: Mateo's Moonlight Adventure
synopsis: When young Mateo feels scared about sleeping in his new big boy bed, he discovers that being brave doesn't mean not feeling scared - it means doing something even when you are scared. With help from his loving family and a magical moonbeam friend, Mateo learns that nighttime can be wonderful and peaceful.

pages.text: Mateo looked at his new big boy bed. It was much bigger than his old crib. "I don't want to sleep there," he said to Mami. "It feels too big and scary."

illustration.description: A young Latino boy with curly dark hair and brown eyes stands next to a wooden twin bed with blue dinosaur sheets. His mother, a warm-faced Latina woman with long dark hair, kneels beside him with a gentle expression. The bedroom has soft yellow walls with star decorations and a nightlight glowing softly.
illustration.alt: Mateo looking worried at his new bed while his mother comforts him

pages.text: Mami hugged Mateo close. "It's okay to feel scared," she said softly. "Big changes can make us feel worried. But you are growing up to be such a brave boy."

illustration.description: Mateo's mother embraces him in a warm hug in the cozy bedroom. Her face shows understanding and love. Mateo's expression is sad but comforted. The room is bathed in warm golden light from a bedside lamp, creating a safe, nurturing atmosphere with soft textures and rounded furniture edges.
illustration.alt: Mami hugging Mateo tenderly in his bedroom

pages.text: That night, Mateo climbed into his big bed. He felt scared and alone. Suddenly, a gentle moonbeam danced through his window. "Hello, Mateo," whispered the moonbeam. "I'm here to keep you company."

illustration.description: Mateo sits up in his big bed looking surprised but curious as a silvery-white moonbeam streams through his window. The moonbeam has a friendly, sparkly face with twinkling eyes. The room is dimly lit in blues and purples, with the moonbeam creating a magical, ethereal glow across the dinosaur-patterned bedsheets.
illustration.alt: A magical moonbeam with a friendly face visiting Mateo in his bed

pages.text: The moonbeam showed Mateo all the wonderful things that happen at night. "Look," it said, "the stars are having a dance party, and the sleepy birds are singing lullabies to their babies."

illustration.description: Through Mateo's window, the night sky comes alive with twinkling stars that seem to dance and sway. In a nearby tree, a diverse family of birds - a cardinal, a blue jay, and a robin - gently sing to their nestlings. The moonbeam illuminates the magical scene with soft, dreamy light in shades of deep blue, silver, and gold.
illustration.alt: Dancing stars and singing birds visible through Mateo's bedroom window

pages.text: Mateo smiled and felt happy watching the nighttime magic. "The night isn't scary at all," he said. "It's beautiful and peaceful. I think I can be brave now."

illustration.description: Mateo sits cross-legged on his bed with a big, joyful smile, pointing excitedly at the window. His face shows wonder and happiness instead of fear. The moonbeam glows warmly beside him like a gentle friend. The room feels cozy and safe, filled with soft blue and silver light that creates a dreamy, comfortable atmosphere.
illustration.alt: Mateo smiling happily and pointing at the beautiful night scene

pages.text: Mateo snuggled under his soft blankets. "Thank you, moonbeam friend," he whispered. "Will you visit me again tomorrow night?" The moonbeam smiled and promised to return every night.

illustration.description: Mateo lies comfortably tucked under his blue dinosaur blankets, looking content and sleepy. The moonbeam hovers nearby with a gentle, caring expression, its light creating a protective glow around the bed. The room is peaceful and quiet, with soft shadows and warm colors creating a perfect bedtime atmosphere.
illustration.alt: Mateo tucked in bed saying goodnight to his moonbeam friend

pages.text: In the morning, Papi found Mateo sleeping peacefully in his big boy bed. "Look how brave our little boy is," Papi whispered to Mami. Mateo woke up feeling proud and happy. He wasn't scared anymore!

illustration.description: Morning sunlight streams through the window as Mateo's father, a Latino man with a beard and kind eyes, and his mother peek into the bedroom. Mateo stretches and yawns with a big smile, sitting up in his bed surrounded by rumpled but cozy bedding. The room is bright and cheerful with golden morning light, showing the family's loving bond.
illustration.alt: Mateo waking up happy while his parents smile proudly in the doorway

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did Mateo feel about his new big boy bed at first?
questions.explanation: Mateo felt scared because the new bed was much bigger than his old crib and felt scary to him.

questions.question: Who helped Mateo feel better about nighttime?
questions.explanation: A magical moonbeam came through his window and showed him all the beautiful things that happen at night.

questions.question: What did the moonbeam show Mateo?
questions.explanation: The moonbeam showed Mateo the stars having a dance party and birds singing lullabies to their babies.

questions.question: How did Mateo feel in the morning?
questions.explanation: Mateo woke up feeling proud and happy because he had been brave and slept in his big boy bed all night.

pages.title: Complete the Story
pages.instructions: Fill in the missing words from Mateo's story!
pages.text: Mateo looked at his new big boy ____. "I don't want to sleep there," he said. "It feels too big and ____." But Mami said it's okay to feel scared, and that Mateo is very ____.

pages.title: Moonbeam Magic
pages.instructions: What did the moonbeam show Mateo?
pages.text: The ____ showed Mateo wonderful things at night. The stars were having a dance ____, and the birds were singing ____ to their babies. Mateo felt ____ watching the nighttime magic.
